title: Two Pointers
subtitle: Walk an array with two indexes

sections.heading: The pattern
sections.body: Many array problems get easier when you walk with **two indexes** instead of one. Often one starts at the front, one at the back, and they move toward each other based on what they see.

sections.title: Two friends meeting in the middle
sections.body: Two friends start at opposite ends of a street. Each takes a step based on a rule. They keep walking until they meet — and along the way they've checked every important pair.
